"The content of the meeting is summarized below:"
what is Statistical inference

Statistical inference refers to the process of drawing conclusions or making predictions about a population based on sample data. It involves using statistical techniques to analyze the sample information and make inferences about the entire population. These inferences may include estimating population parameters, testing hypotheses, or making predictions about future observations. Statistical inference plays a crucial role in various fields, including scientific research, business, economics, and social sciences.
what is central limit theorem

The central limit theorem is a fundamental concept in statistics. It states that, under certain conditions, the sampling distribution of the mean of a random sample from any population will approximate a normal distribution, regardless of the shape of the population distribution. In other words, as the sample size increases, the distribution of sample means will converge to a bell-shaped normal distribution.

The central limit theorem allows statisticians to make inferences about a population based on a relatively small sample. It is used extensively in hypothesis testing, confidence interval estimation, and various statistical analyses. The theorem is essential because it enables us to apply statistical techniques that rely on the assumption of normality, even when the data may not follow a normal distribution.
